feed.title: SAP Community - Machine Learning
feed.subtitle: Machine Learning Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: 1MV Cash Application Machine Learning Model - How to get 5,000-10,000 cleared payments in one Model
entry.content:
Dear Sir or Madam,

The following question has arisen when using machine learning to post-process the bank statement.

This documentation https://me.sap.com/notes/0003001671 is pointing out that 5,000 -10,000 cleared payments must be available in order to train the ML model sufficiently.

To do this, we carry out the following steps:

  1. ML_CASH_APP_JOB_STATUS_DP and set status to “In Progress” (P_INPROG)
  2. ML_CASH_APP_DATA_POST to extract training data
  3. ML_CASH_APP_JOB_STATUS_DP and set status to “Complete” (P_COMPL) -> This will trigger training in cloud

The Model Manager for SAP Cash Application can now be found in the BTP Cockpit:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_CASH_APPLICATION/9668f05979b644cd960f0d83ae4ed0aa/73fd86f7258b46bf84ef47f415af18fa.html?locale=en-US

When steps 1 to 3 are executed, a new inactive model is created. With regard to the processed items, the statistics show that not all cleared payments from the ERP are processed cumulatively. We can recognize this by the number of items when we run this training job every month. The number does not accumulate. In addition, each training run creates a new model that must then be activated.

How are we supposed to obtain an ML model that has generated 5,000-10,000 cleared payments if each training run only processes a part or new cleared payments and then generates a new model on this basis?

Who can explain this in detail?

Thank you very much
Sebastian

entry.title: CAP deployment error of AI mission
entry.content:
Dear guys,

I try to reproduce the mission "Develop a CAP-based (multitenant) application using GenAI and RAG" in my environment. (single tenant on cloud foundry)
During the deployment step I got this error message:

I had to remove the {org-id} from the xsuaa-app name in mta.yaml file because our org-id contains spaces and does not fit the naming conventions. I just added a string "-kps".

Any ideas or hints what needs to be done to get it working?

Br
Wolfgang

entry.title: AI/ML Functionality in SAP IBP Inventory Optimization
entry.content:
Hi All,

Would like to gain an insight on the AI/ML functionality in the Inventory Optimization module of SAP IBP if there are any. Any leads or any Help portal links would help. If any functionality exists will be trying to check the changes in the outputs when using AI for the Inventory Optimization run.

Thanks in Advance

Purrab

SAP Integrated Business Planning for inventory Artificial Intelligence Machine Learning

entry.title: Where to deploy RAG application for SAP Gen AI Hub
entry.content:
Hi,

What are the best practices recommended by SAP regarding hosting the RAG application that is used to provide context for the LLM model being setup in SAP Gen AI Hub?

In one of the SAP training sessions for SAP Generative AI Hub, the SAP trainers had introduced using Business Application Studio (BAS) as the development platform. For the RAG application that is being developed in BAS, must it be containerized through Docker and deployed it in Cloud Foundry to be consume by some other SAP services e.g. SAP Build App? Is there any alternative to this?

Thank you.

entry.title: Can I add rows to an existing SAP HANA table from a python dataframe?
entry.content: hana_ml.dataframe.create_dataframe_from_pandas() allows me to create a new SAP HANA table from the data of a python dataframe. However if that HANA table already exists and contains data, I just want to add more rows to it. Is that also possible? Or do I have to create a temporary table via create_dataframe_from_pandas and then move the rows to the real table?

entry.title: Incorrect path when activating an environment
entry.content:
Hello experts,
I am currently working through this very interesting blog post. However, when installing Python or activating the environment, I encounter the following error ‘The system cannot find the specified path’.

I would be very happy to receive help from the experts here.

Many thanks
Patric

entry.title: How can I parallelize the training of a HANA_ML MLPRegressor?
entry.content:
According to the documentation setting thread_ratio=1 means all available threads on SAP HANA will be used for training, but I can only see 5 threads of the scriptserver being active. This is exactly the same behavior as when training with the default value of thread_ratio=0. How can I speed up the training via parallelization?

from hana_ml.algorithms.pal.neural_network import MLPRegressor

mlp = MLPRegressor(hidden_layer_size=(layer1,layer2),
activation='relu',
output_activation='relu',
max_iter=iterations,
weight_init='normal',
training_style='batch',
normalization='z-transform',
thread_ratio=1)

mlp.fit(train_df, features=x_columns, label='y')

entry.title: Custom Field not getting updated
entry.content:
Custom field ethnicity/Race is configured to sync from EC into LMS through the connector, checking the archives in connector schedulling, the field does come from but when you go to the Label on the profile in LMS it is not showing. 

sfuser.extendeduser.field.mapping.data.COL_NUM3_VAL=OCCUPATIONAL_LEVEL
sfuser.extendeduser.field.mapping.data.COL_NUM4_VAL=CHALLENGE_STATUS
sfuser.extendeduser.field.mapping.data.COL_NUM5_VAL=ETHNICITY
sfuser.extendeduser.field.mapping.data.COL_NUM7_VAL=RISK_LOSS
sfuser.extendeduser.field.mapping.data.COL_NUM8_VAL=IMPACT_LOSS

@PL1    

@Harish_Vatsa 
 
SF-Dan

entry.title: Missing Execution ID in SAP AI Launchpad After Connecting AWS S3 Bucket via Object Store Secret
entry.content: I'm using SAP AI Launchpad. I created an Object Store Secret and connected it to my bucket in AWS, as it is a demo the S3 has admin role privilege in order tu access and has an access point, but in my dataset in SAP AI Launchpad, I don't see an execution ID. What can I do?

entry.title: Sci-Kit learn library in SAP Datasphere Data Flow
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I tried to add Python code into a Sap Datasphere Data Flow utilizing the sci-kit learn library. However I always get errors. Does anyone know if the library is available in the Dataflow Script Console or what alteratives I would have here?

Many thanks,

Moritz

entry.title: Access event log, case attributes via API to perform ML based predictions
entry.content:
Hi Team, 

I am looking for options to access Business objects, event log created within Process Intelligence. I would require to access this data to perform ML based predictions or employ other AI based applications.  

The idea is to perform all these computations in external notebook and push this data back to Process Intelligence so that we can show predictions or any other applications.

Has anyone worked on this kind of setup, how to access data from Process Intelligence and push it back to the model. Any kind of API provided or Python based setup available, please advise.

@Process Intelligence @SAP Signavio

entry.title: Joule Extensibility POC for SAP Learning
entry.content:
Hello everyone,
We're considering a proof of concept that involves utilizing the Joule Extensibility feature in relation to the SAP Learning platform (Learn SAP skills | SAP Learning). The idea is to implement a chatbot that enhances user experience by offering improved recommendations and roadmaps based on their search queries. I'm curious about the feasibility of this concept, given that Joule can currently be integrated only with SAP Line of Business products. Is it possible for us to proceed with this use case?

entry.title: How to enhance Machine Learning for C4C v1
entry.content:
Hi - we have a new requirement where the ML for Service should only work for specific Ticket Type and Channel. I know we have Enhancement Spot for ML but not sure where to start.

Thanks!

Marc

entry.title: Deployment is in Pending Status since 3 days in SAP AI launchpad - ML Deployment
entry.content:
Greetings Community Members!

Kindly help me with understanding on Deployment troubleshooting.  I have deployed a vision model using Postman, it was working well then I made some changes in re-deployed again. It has been in pending status since last 3 days.  Is there any ways to know Logs, debug, or troubleshoot deployment process ? in AI launchpad - ML( Deployment Section) Logs are very basic not providing more information,  is there any way we can see more detail logs for the deployment ?

Thank you
Best Regards
Naim 

 SAP AI Core

 # AI Launchpad 

#SAP ML Deployments

entry.title: Where to maintain ISLM Connection mapping in S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content: I was following multiple blogs to understand what is ISLM. There was a place where they were doing configuration in ISLM Connection mapping in SPRO. How to do that in S/4HANA Public Cloud version.

entry.title: Unable to create Intelligent Scenarios in S/4 Public Cloud
entry.content:
When I try to create a Intelligent Scenario in S/4 HANA Public Cloud, I am getting below error. 
Unable to edit the field.

entry.title: Questions about SAP Joule
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

We are planning to enable SAP Joule. But before we proceed, we wanted to understand the capabilities of Joule with respect to SAP Sales Cloud Version 2. I had a few questions regarding it, please read through them and provide your insights.

I understand that SAP Joule currently has 3 Interaction Patterns: Transactional, Navigational and Informational. But I would like to know more about the following:

1. What are the Business Objects supported in Joule with respect to SAP Sales Cloud Version 2?
2. What are the use-cases that are supported in Joule with respect to SAP Sales Cloud Version 2?
3. What are the current limitations of Joule?
4. Is there a way to customize navigation capabilities of Joule from SAP Sales Cloud Version 2 to SAP ECC?

If there are any online sources that I could reference for the questions above, please provide those to me as well.

Thanks in advance,
Karthik C M.

entry.title: Detecting Handwritten vs. Digital Docs in SAP CAP + DOX
entry.content: I'm working on a solution using SAP CAP (Cloud Application Programming) with Node.js, integrated with SAP Document Information Extraction (DOX). This project sends documents via API to the DOX service to extract relevant information from files such as invoices and receipts. My requirement here is to check whether the document is handwritten or computer-generated. This distinction could significantly enhance data accuracy and processing logic. If anyone has insights on integrating handwriting detection with SAP DOX or using pre-processing techniques for document classification, I’d love to connect and learn more.

entry.title: The AI Adoption Journey: Machine Learning- A Deeper Dive!
entry.content:
Hello all!

Last blog, we skimmed over some of the Machine Learning features that SAP Fieldglass has but I’ve since gotten to experiment with it more and I must talk about it!
First things first. Machine Learning is a part of the base AI for Fieldglass, which means its available for anyone that has Fieldglass. It blows my mind that it’s not used more!

So, why would anyone want to use these features? Like the example I gave in the last blog, getting a large influx of resumes from various job postings can be a substantial burden on your administrative team.

How does Resume Assessment work to resolve this? AI assists in identifying the right job seekers by screening submitted candidates in how close of a match they are to the job description.
I remember when I was starting college, the advice was to put all the important information around the middle of the page, where a person’s thumb would be holding it. This was the idea they used to have about “efficiency” when it came to reviewing resumes. I'm sure we've all heard little tips and tricks like that- repurposing keywords in the resumes, placing them somewhere specific on the page, even hacks on fonts to keep the readers eyes on the page! 
Now, we have AI that will comb through the candidate resumes to do a match on both skills and work experience.
Your business has more sway in this process than you might think! Buyers can prioritize the factors differently, depending on industry, job title or level of experience! A new field called Resume Assessment Weighting on the Contingent Type can be leveraged to indicate the level of importance the business deems appropriate.
This field contains four main categories that the buyer can set based on their preference to produce a banded score: Skills, Industry Experience, Work Experience and Qualifications.

The algorithm matches the skills on the resume The algorithm matches the skills listed on the resume against the application's skills library, analyzes the industry experience, work experience, and qualifications based on the preferences set using the Resume Assessment Weighting field, and produces that banded score. This scoring system uses a five-tier band: best, better, good, weak, no match) to indicate compatibility with the job posting description.
So why the banded score? This high-level score can let the administrative team know, immediately, who the most compatible candidates are for the Job Postings.  With this feature in use, businesses saw that 61% of candidates hired were ranked “Best” by resume assessment.
ML doesn’t stop here with the requisition process. There’s also an experience assessment and skills extraction The Experience Assessment will offer a percentile score (1-100%) based on skills and related experience, as well as skills extraction, where AI combs through the submitted resume, and will visually highlight skills and qualifications making them easier to see (freeing up your thumbs and saving paper). This not only speeds up the reviewing, short listing and hiring process. Customers that enabled this feature saw an over all 15% reduction in workers closed with a “do not hire” reason.

These seemingly simple change in automation removes bottlenecks in the process in speeding up review to hire but also cleans up the integrity of the workforce hired overall.

Of course, there is all this information and so much more to play with in an interactive setting on the SAP Fieldglass Product Work Zone. They have an amazing AI Hub that I strongly encourage you to explore: SAP Fieldglass Product Success Work Zone - AI Hub

Also, please check out this great series by Fabian Caelers on Quick Win Features to learn some more valuable tips and tricks to make the most of your solution: SAP Fieldglass - Quick Win Features - Episode 2 - SAP Community

Join me next week, as I get into the obscure gem that is O*NET code recommendations in Fieldglass and why that matters!

entry.title: SAP BDC Demo Environment
entry.content:
Hello All, 

Since the BDC trial version is now available, I’d like to create a demo using it. Could you please guide me on how to enable or build the content? Also, is it possible to showcase a demo using standard content, considering that the source systems are not properly maintained? I’m looking for any way to effectively pitch it to clients. I’m exploring options to work exclusively within BDC AI/ML part. The practice source systems are not properly maintained, making it impractical to establish connections with them. However, intelligent applications or insight apps require both source and target systems to be connected in order to enable the content. Are there any materials or documents available that can guide the implementation of Databricks AI/ML on raw data within an SAP BDC environment?
